sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: 5 Overbought Tech Stocks to Sell for Profit
feed.summary: This article identifies five overbought tech stocks that investors should consider selling for profit, despite the ongoing earnings season and strong performance in the tech sector. These companies — MaxLinear Inc., Texas Instruments Inc., Atomera Inc., Skywater Technology Inc., and ARM Holdings PLC — show signs of overvaluation or waning momentum according to technical indicators like RSI and MACD, suggesting impending pullbacks. The piece advises long-term shareholders to take profits now given the extended rallies and potential for corrections.

feed.title: Leidos (LDOS) Secures $869M Contract to Enhance Pentagon Decision-Making Systems
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings Inc. has been awarded an $869 million, five-year contract by the U.S. Army under the MACRO II program to develop secure systems using AI and modern networking. This contract is expected to enhance military operational capabilities and position Leidos as a key defense technology player. Despite insider selling activity, the company exhibits strong profitability, growth, and a potentially undervalued stock with a GF Score™ of 86/100.

feed.title: [SCHEDULE 13G] VERTEX PHARMACEUTICALS INC / MA Passive Investment Disclosure (>5%)
feed.summary: Vanguard Capital Management has filed a Schedule 13G for Vertex Pharmaceuticals Inc (VRTX), reporting beneficial ownership of 19,036,076 shares of Common Stock, which represents 7.49% of the class as of March 31, 2026. The filing indicates Vanguard's passive investment approach, with sole voting power over 2,523,266 shares and sole dispositive power over all 19,036,076 shares. This disclosure highlights a significant institutional stake without implying an intent for active control of the company.

feed.title: Amex Gold adds new benefits for 60th anniversary: Rental car status, additional dining partners and more
feed.summary: The American Express Gold Card is celebrating its 60th anniversary with new benefits and limited-time offers, without an increase in its annual fee. Cardholders will enjoy boosted Amex Travel earnings for prepaid hotels, complimentary Hertz Five Star Status, and an expanded list of dining partners for the monthly credit. Additionally, there are temporary offers including a credit for Uber One membership for Gold cardholders and special deals at The Hotel Collection for both Gold and Platinum cardholders.

feed.title: Bangladesh inks deal to buy 14 US Boeing aircraft
feed.summary: Bangladesh has signed a deal with the US Boeing Company to purchase 14 aircraft for its national flag carrier, Biman Bangladesh Airlines. The agreement includes 10 wide-body 787 Dreamliners and four narrow-body 737 MAX aircraft, estimated to cost around $3.7 billion. This procurement aims to expand the airline's fleet, strengthen Bangladesh's position in the global aviation market, and enhance connectivity, with deliveries expected between October 2031 and November 2035.

feed.title: Is Synchrony Financial (SYF) Attractive After Strong Multi Year Share Price Performance
feed.summary: Synchrony Financial (SYF) has shown strong multi-year share price performance, despite recent short-term fluctuations. Simply Wall St's analysis suggests the stock is undervalued, with an intrinsic value estimate of $152.12 per share based on the Excess Returns model and a P/E ratio below the industry average. The article presents bullish and bearish narratives to help investors evaluate the stock's potential based on different growth and risk assumptions.

feed.title: Qualcomm Earnings: Data Center Optimism Overshadows Handset Weakness
feed.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) reported mixed fiscal second-quarter results, with revenue down 3% year-over-year and a forecast for a further 7% decline in the June quarter due to handset weakness. However, the stock rallied 13% after hours on news of a "leading hyperscaler custom silicon engagement" for AI chips starting in late 2026. Despite limited details, this data center optimism and strong automotive chip growth helped to overshadow the ongoing challenges in the handset market, leading Morningstar to maintain its $155 fair value estimate while adjusting revenue predictions.

The filing details Vanguard's beneficial ownership and dispositive power through its affiliates and managed funds.

feed.title: ASML Holding NV Stock (ASML) Moved Up by 3.25% on Apr 30: Drivers Behind the Movement
feed.summary: ASML Holding NV (ASML) stock rose by 3.25% on April 30, driven by strong Q1 2026 financial results that exceeded expectations and an increased full-year revenue outlook. The surge in demand for advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment, particularly from the AI sector, is a key driver, leading to more orders for ASML's EUV lithography systems. Analyst sentiment is largely positive, with several firms reaffirming or upgrading their ratings and price targets, indicating confidence in the company's growth trajectory despite some intraday volatility.

feed.title: Atkore International Group Inc (ATKR) Stock Price Today & Analysis
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of Atkore International Group Inc (ATKR) stock, including its current price, fundamental financials, and corporate actions such as dividend yield. It describes Atkore Inc. as a manufacturer of electrical products and safety & infrastructure products for construction and industrial markets. The piece also includes a FAQ section on how to buy ATKR stock on the Gotrade app, minimum investment, dividend policy, safety, market hours, and how to assess if the stock is expensive or cheap.

feed.title: Bangladesh's flag carrier picks 14 Boeing jets to expand routes
feed.summary: Biman Bangladesh Airlines has placed its largest-ever order with Boeing for 14 new jets, including 787-10 Dreamliners, 787-9 Dreamliners, and 737-8 MAX aircraft. This acquisition aims to modernize the airline's fleet, increase passenger and cargo capacity, and expand its international route network, with a focus on destinations in the Middle East, Europe, North America, India, and Southeast Asia. The new aircraft are expected to offer a 20-25% improvement in fuel efficiency compared to the planes they replace, enhancing operational performance.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase (NYSE: JPM) offers 5.25% callable notes maturing 2036
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is offering callable fixed-rate notes due May 15, 2036, with an annual interest rate of 5.25%. These unsecured notes are callable semiannually by the issuer from May 2028 through November 2035. Investors should be aware of the credit risk of JPMorgan Chase and the potential for early redemption, which could lead to reinvestment risk.

feed.title: FedEx confirms elimination of 'certain positions'
feed.summary: FedEx has acknowledged the elimination of "certain positions" as part of its ongoing cost-cutting and restructuring efforts. This move is consistent with the company's recent strategies aimed at improving efficiency and financial performance. The Memphis Business Journal reported on the confirmation of these job cuts.

feed.title: ADP Earnings: AI-Driven Efficiency and Stronger Pricing Power Fuel Guidance Raise
feed.summary: Automatic Data Processing (ADP) reported strong Q3 results with revenue up 7% and adjusted EBIT margin expanding. The company raised its full-year fiscal 2026 guidance, citing improved client funds interest balances, higher retention, and stronger pricing power, partly driven by AI-powered efficiencies. Morningstar maintains its $231 fair value for ADP, seeing the stock as undervalued despite a post-earnings pop.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Raises Ameriprise Financial (NYSE:AMP) Price Target to $467.00
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has increased its price target for Ameriprise Financial (NYSE:AMP) to $467.00 from $452.00, though it maintains an "underweight" rating, suggesting a potential downside. This comes after Ameriprise beat Q1 EPS estimates, reporting $11.26 against an expected $10.21, despite revenue slightly missing expectations at $4.77 billion. The financial services provider currently holds a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average price target of $542.00.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Fortive Q1 2026 beats EPS forecast; stock dips
feed.summary: Fortive Corporation reported strong Q1 2026 results, surpassing EPS forecasts with $0.70 against an expected $0.64, and achieving a 7.9% year-over-year revenue increase to $1.1 billion. Despite the positive earnings surprise, the stock declined by 4.78% in pre-market trading, potentially due to tariff-related pressures on gross margins and valuation concerns. The company reaffirmed its full-year EPS guidance of $2.90 to $3.00, expecting to trend towards the upper half of that range, driven by strategic initiatives in AI-driven solutions and disciplined capital allocation including significant share repurchases.

feed.title: Camden Property Trust (NYSE: CPT) renews $500M at-the-market program
feed.summary: Camden Property Trust (NYSE: CPT) has renewed its at-the-market (ATM) equity program, allowing it to offer and sell up to $500 million in common shares. The program provides financing flexibility through direct share issuance and forward sale agreements with major banks. Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including potentially repaying its $1.2 billion unsecured revolving credit facility and other debt.

feed.title: Board of Directors of The Coca-Cola Company Elects Two New Officers and Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: The Board of Directors of The Coca-Cola Company announced the election of Sedef Salingan Sahin as executive vice president and Chief Digital Officer, and Tapaswee Chandele as executive vice president and Global Chief People Officer. The company also declared a regular quarterly dividend of 53 cents per common share, payable July 1 to shareowners of record as of June 15. The announcement highlights the company's commitment to leadership development and shareholder returns.

feed.title: KKR & Co. Set to Report Q1 Earnings: What's in Store for the Stock?
feed.summary: KKR & Co. Inc. (KKR) is scheduled to release its first-quarter 2026 results on May 5, 2026, with analysts expecting year-over-year increases in earnings and revenues. Despite missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate last quarter due to higher expenses, the firm has a strong earnings surprise history, driven by growth in assets under management (AUM). Recent strategic moves, including the acquisition of Arctos Partners and divestitures from Janney Montgomery Scott LLC, are expected to further streamline its focus on alternative investment and asset management sectors.

feed.title: Amazon Stock Falls On Earnings Despite AWS 'Growing At Its Fastest Clip In 4 Years'
feed.summary: Amazon's stock fell despite reporting strong first-quarter financial results driven by its AWS segment, which showed its fastest growth in four years. Analysts from Goldman Sachs, Scotiabank, Rosenblatt, JPMorgan, and Needham reiterated Buy or Outperform ratings and raised their price targets, citing AWS's acceleration, strong backlog, and AI integration. The article highlights Amazon's competitive advantages in hardware and business mix, with Prime Video also achieving profitability for the first time.

This disclosure provides transparency on significant ownership in Sysco.

feed.title: Everest signals $300M quarterly buyback floor for 2026 as retail exit drives about $150M restructuring charges
feed.summary: This article reports that Everest (EVR) plans a significant $300 million quarterly share buyback program starting in 2026. This initiative comes as the company faces approximately $150 million in restructuring charges, largely driven by its exit from the retail market. The buyback signals a strategic shift towards enhancing shareholder value while streamlining its business operations.

feed.title: Amtech Systems Appoints Veteran Thomas Sabol as CFO
feed.summary: Amtech Systems (ASYS) has appointed Thomas Sabol, a finance veteran with over three decades of experience, as its new Chief Financial Officer, effective May 14, 2026. His compensation package includes a $350,000 base salary, annual bonus eligibility, and 45,000 stock options, aligning his interests with shareholder value. While Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates ASYS as Neutral due to weak profitability and declining revenue, it notes positive cash generation, managerial leverage, and strong technicals as partial offsets.

feed.title: Valero to run refineries up to 95% of capacity in Q2 2026, conf call
feed.summary: Valero Energy Corp plans to operate its 13 refineries in the U.S. and Britain at 92%-95% of their combined 3 million bpd capacity in the second quarter of 2026. This includes the restart of its Port Arthur, Texas refinery after a March explosion and fire, and accounts for the permanent closure of its Benicia, California refinery. The company anticipates high refining margins for six months to a year due to global supply disruptions and reported strong first-quarter earnings, exceeding analyst expectations.

feed.title: Passenger accused of opening cabin door on Delta flight from Atlanta, faces federal charge
feed.summary: A Delta Air Lines passenger, Thomas W. Ryan, is facing a federal charge for allegedly interfering with a flight crew after attempting to open a cabin door on a flight from Atlanta to Chicago. The incident occurred after a series of delays, causing Ryan to become aggressive and demand to deplane. The flight returned to the gate, and Ryan was taken into custody by Atlanta police.

feed.title: JPMorgan hires KBW's Mihok as MD in banking team
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase has appointed Chris Mihok from Keefe, Bruyette & Woods as a managing director to oversee coverage of banks, enhancing its financial institutions team. Mihok will report to Clay Robinson and Andrew Spicehandler, who lead North America banks, and is set to begin his role in early July. JPMorgan has recently advised on significant deals, including the takeover of Britain's Schroders by Nuveen and Santander's acquisition of U.S. regional lender Webster.

feed.title: Avidia Bancorp EVP Karmelek buys $25,327 in common stock
feed.summary: Nicolas Karmelek, Executive Vice President and Chief Risk Officer at Avidia Bancorp, recently purchased 1,220 shares of the company's common stock for $25,327. This transaction occurred on April 28, 2026, with the stock trading near its 52-week high after a significant gain. Avidia Bancorp also reported increased first-quarter 2026 net income and announced a quarterly cash dividend.

feed.title: Single-use Bioprocessing Market Size Advancing at 11.0% CAGR |
feed.summary: The single-use bioprocessing market is projected to reach $55.6 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 16.5% from its 2024 size of $14.8 billion. This growth is driven by increasing biologics production, vaccine manufacturing scale-up, and the need for flexible, cost-efficient systems in a volatile global trade environment, especially in light of geopolitical disruptions. Artificial intelligence and continuous bioprocessing are key technological trends optimizing operations and attracting significant investment, particularly in North America and the rapidly expanding Asia-Pacific region.
